zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Fuller於1572年10月19日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可托普可洛福特時，他的父親John Fuller II19歲，母親Anne Harwyne22歲。他於1600年4月24日與Jane Gowan在英國諾福爾可雷登哈爾 煙雨 哈爾斯頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1638年在英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可托普可洛福特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Fuller于1572年10月19日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可托普可洛福特时，他的父亲John Fuller II19岁，母亲Anne Harwyne22岁。他于1600年4月24日与Jane Gowan在英国諾福爾可雷登哈爾 煙雨 哈爾斯頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1638年在英国艾可傑爾姆斯福德享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可托普可洛福特。

nameMeanings.name: Fuller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walker
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Filler
meanings: South German: occupational name for a flayer or skinner, from Middle High German viller ‘knacker’, from villen ‘to skin’.

variants.name: Fullen
meanings: English: occupational name for a fuller (see Fuller ), from Old French fulun ‘fuller’.
English (Lancashire): branch of this family in Lancashire probably represents a variant of Fulham (see Fullam ), reintroduced from Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Fuller was born on 19 October 1572, in Topcroft, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Fuller II, was 19 and his mother, Anne Harwyne, was 22. He married Jane Gowan on 24 April 1600, in Redenhall with Harleston, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He died about 1638, in Chelmsford, Essex, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Topcroft, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Fuller est né le 19 octobre 1572, à Topcroft, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Fuller II, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Anne Harwyne, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Jane Gowan le 24 avril 1600, à Redenhall with Harleston, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé environ 1638, à Chelmsford, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Topcroft, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Fullerが1572年10月19日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクトプクロフトで生まれた時，父John Fuller IIは19歳，母Anne Harwyneは22歳であった。1600年4月24日にイギリスノーフォルクレデンハル ウィス ハーレストンでJane Gowanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は約1638年にイギリスエセックスチェルムスフォードで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクトプクロフトにて埋葬された。

ko.Redenhall, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 레데홀 위스 하를레스턴
ko.lifeSummary: Roger Fuller(이)가 1572년 10월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 토p크로프트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Fuller II(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Harwyne(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1600년 4월 24일 영국 노르폴크 레데홀 위스 하를레스턴에서 Jane Gowan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1638년 영국 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 토p크로프트에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Jane Gowan em 24 de abril de 1600, em Redenhall with Harleston, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. ru.Redenhall, Norfolk, England: реденолл вит харльстон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Fuller родился 19 октября 1572 г. в местности топкрофт, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Fuller II было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Harwyne, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Gowan 24 апреля 1600 г. в местности реденолл вит харльстон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер около 1638 г. в местности челмсфорд, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности топкрофт, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
